doc_title: Navigating the Safety Landscape: Measuring Risks in Finetuning Large Language Models

items.fact: LLMs and Sec. 3.4 introduces 'LLM safety basin' concept and proposes the VISAGE safety metric based on our landscape analysis.
items.evidence_span: LLMs and Sec. 3.4 introduces 'LLM safety basin' concept and proposes the VISAGE safety metric based on our landscape analysis.

items.fact: We use LLaMA2 as an example and show the safety landscape of 1D-interpolation LLaMA2-7B → LLaMA2-7B-chat in Fig. 2a.
items.evidence_span: We use LLaMA2 as an example and show the safety landscape of 1D-interpolation LLaMA2-7B → LLaMA2-7B-chat in Fig. 2a.

items.fact: For each perturbed model along the landscape direction, we evaluate on the first 80 prompts of AdvBench [42] 'Harmful Behaviors' split (Adv 80) with ASR as the safety metric.
items.evidence_span: For each perturbed model along the landscape direction, we evaluate on the first 80 prompts of AdvBench [42] 'Harmful Behaviors' split (Adv 80) with ASR as the safety metric.

items.fact: Note that S can be any harmfulness evaluation metric, e.g., LLM Judge [41] or Llama Guard [19].
items.evidence_span: Note that S can be any harmfulness evaluation metric, e.g., LLM Judge [41] or Llama Guard [19].
We finetune on the harmful samples created by Qi et al. [29],
which were sampled from Anthropic red-teaming dataset [14].
Following the standard OpenAI finetuning API [28],
safe data created by Bianchi et al. [4]
This is likely due to LLaMA3's improved training procedures, which substantially reduce false refusal rates [2].
This is likely due to LLaMA3's improved training procedures, which substantially reduce false refusal rates [2].
We use the adversarial prompts from JailbreakBench [9], which has incorporated jailbreaking prompts targeting LLaMA2 and Vicuna generated by GCG [42] and PAIR [8] adversarial attacks.
